description_long: This is a military-style, twin-engine propeller airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring green and brown patches with roundel insignias on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft has a streamlined fuselage, a glass-enclosed cockpit positioned near the front, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with visible dihedral angles and no winglets. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The propellers are mounted on the leading edge of each wing, and the aircraft displays markings typical of World War II-era British planes. The overall design is robust, with smooth, hard surfaces and a metallic sheen.
description: A twin-propeller, camouflage-painted military airplane with a glass cockpit, mid-mounted straight wings, and British roundel markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, glass cockpit, and symmetrical wing layout.
description_view_1: Left-side perspective highlighting the camouflage pattern, roundel insignia, and tail markings.
description_view_2: Rear-front view displaying the twin engines, cockpit canopy, and tail structure.
description_view_3: Right-side perspective showing the propellers, fuselage markings, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with moderate dihedral angle and no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings with visible propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear, likely tricycle configuration (not visible in images).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel glass canopy.
